mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the real retail sales series RSAFS reflects strengthening consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings expectations upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The effect is monotonic and always-on, requiring no threshold trigger.
failure_condition: The 12-month rolling correlation between RSAFS monthly growth and next-20-day SPX returns is negative or below 0.05 for any consecutive 2-year period in the walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate UNRATE falls to a cycle low below 4.0%, it signals a tight labor market that puts upward pressure on wages and consumer spending, supporting earnings growth and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal activates only when the threshold is breached.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward SPX return during UNRATE < 4% periods is not above the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point (t-test p < 0.10) over the entire sample.

mechanism_narrative: The joint rise in both FEDFUNDS (tightening monetary policy) and UNRATE (deteriorating labor market) creates a stagflationary signal that compresses equity valuation multiples and lowers aggregate demand expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The interaction effect is not decomposable into separate single-series effects.
failure_condition: In a linear regression of SPX forward returns on FEDFUNDS, UNRATE, and their interaction, the interaction coefficient is not negative and significant at the 10% level over any 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation measured by CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%, further increases in the price level signal persistent overheating that compels the Federal Reserve to continue or accelerate policy tightening, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward SPX return during high-inflation trigger periods is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p < 0.05) compared to periods when CPI YoY is below 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts HOUST (12-month change below -10%) signals a severe contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, foreshadowing a broader economic slowdown that reduces corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 50% of months following the trigger, or the average return is not below -0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in both industrial production INDPRO and nonfarm payroll employment PAYEMS signals synchronous expansion in output and labor demand, which jointly boost aggregate income and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The effect is driven by the co-movement of the two series.
failure_condition: A model including PAYEMS and INDPRO individually does not have lower out-of-sample RMSE than a model with their interaction; or the interaction coefficient is not positive and significant at 10% level over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0% and is rising month-over-month, further increases compress equity risk premiums and discount future cash flows more heavily, leading to short-term negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice tightening expectations.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return during activated periods is not negative or is indistinguishable from the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent increases in industrial production INDPRO reflect rising capacity utilization and output growth, which improve corporate profitability and signal economic expansion, thereby supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This always-on trend signal requires no threshold activation.
failure_condition: The rolling 5-year Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO monthly growth and next-60-day SPX return is less than 0.10 at any point in the test period.
